title: Idempotency keys vs natural keys
description: A client idempotency key dedups retries of the same intent; a natural key dedups by business uniqueness.
body: Use when choosing how to dedup an operation. A client-supplied idempotency key (header/UUID per attempt) collapses retries of one intent and is best for create-style calls where no natural uniqueness exists. A natural key (order number, email+date) enforces business uniqueness regardless of client. Do not mix them or rely on timestamps that differ per retry. Actions: pick the key, back it with a unique index written in the same transaction as the effect, and return the stored result on conflict. Validation signals include a concurrent duplicate test resolving to one row. Risk boundary: a TTL that expires keys faster than clients retry reopens the double-effect window.
